scope_boundary: Only the bounded Project Theseus RLDS/Minari trace-export import moves to prototype-backed: a sanitized public-safe fixture records one READY export manifest with three declared formats, seven declared fields, required license metadata, required replay smoke, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, no copied episode payload, and seven expected-invalid controls. This does not promote the Resource Economics chapter core claim, prove RLDS dataset correctness, Minari dataset quality, simulator adequacy, replay success, physical feasibility, benchmark transfer, model quality, economic outcome, clean live Project Theseus replay, deployment readiness, safety, alignment, transfer, or ASI.
transition_reason: The imported Theseus report records a concrete trace-export readiness boundary rather than only a proposed simulation-data vocabulary: the source report is READY, the public-safe fixture preserves the exact source digest, one export id, RLDS/Minari-oriented formats, seven required episode fields, license-metadata and replay-smoke requirements, and explicit public-safety/non-claim boundaries. That is enough to accept the narrow import claim as prototype-backed while keeping dataset correctness, replay success, simulator adequacy, model quality, and chapter-core claims at argument.
required_artifacts: sanitized public-safe Theseus RLDS/Minari trace-export fixture
license metadata and replay-smoke gate fields

negative controls for source hash mismatch, private-payload copying, not-ready status, missing license metadata, missing replay-smoke requirement, support-promotion overclaim, and dataset-quality overclaim
negative_results: source report hash mismatch is rejected
private payload or episode payload copying is rejected
missing license metadata requirement is rejected
dataset-quality, simulator-adequacy, and replay-success overclaims are rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: the sanitized fixture no longer validates against the recorded source report digest, source checkout boundary, export count, format list, field list, license metadata requirement, replay-smoke requirement, public-safety boundary, or non-claim boundary

a source hash mismatch, private payload copy, not-ready export, missing license metadata requirement, missing replay-smoke requirement, support promotion overclaim, or dataset-quality overclaim is accepted as valid

the imported report is described as RLDS dataset correctness, Minari dataset quality, simulator adequacy, replay success, physical feasibility, benchmark transfer, model quality, economic outcome, clean live Project Theseus replay, deployment readiness, safety, ASI, or chapter-core evidence
future public dependency policy requires archived raw report publication and this record remains a digest-only sanitized summary
promotion_burden: Move beyond this bounded prototype-backed import only after a public-safe archived Theseus report bundle or clean live replay is available, raw/private trace publication boundaries are resolved, exported RLDS/Minari artifacts can be externally inspected, replay smoke is executed and recorded, dataset-quality and simulator-adequacy checks are separately reviewed, residuals and non-claims are preserved, and independent review is recorded with a separate accepted transition.
